expected_signature.expected_value: visual coincidence of loaded zeros with crossings of |ζ(1/2 + it)|
expected_signature.pass_rule: the polar trace passes through zero at every loaded zero position
expected_signature.tolerance: visual / numerical alignment within rendering precision

baseline_hypothesis.plain_statement: The polar trace correctly visualizes ζ(1/2 + it) and the loaded zero positions over the displayed t-window.
baseline_hypothesis.why_this_matters: This is the entry-point visualization. If it is wrong, every downstream interpretation of zero positions is at risk of being misread.

candidate_lemmas.name: Polar-Trace Demonstration Note (no-lemma)
candidate_lemmas.statement: This experiment is visualization-typed; no candidate lemma is suggested from this run.
what_it_does_not_prove: this is a substitute for any other experiment

disallowed_conclusions: this is a substitute for any other experiment

Zero positions are verified to ζ(ρ)=0 to any specific precision by this experiment.
Coverage extends beyond the displayed t-range.
The compressed and uncompressed dual-window curves agree exactly under any non-trivial multiplicative gauge.
intended_inference_if_passed: the visualization is faithful on the tested window
downstream readers may rely on it for orientation, not for proof

raw_observations.verifier_interpretation: Polar trace rendered with 500 samples and 10 zero markers in range. Dual-window overlay rendered with 200 samples per window.
